title: Streams: Async Iterators
content:
A Stream is the async version of Iterator. Where Iterator::next() returns Option<T> synchronously, Stream::poll_next() returns Poll<Option<T>> and can await between items.

Streams are used for: reading lines from a file asynchronously, receiving messages from a channel, handling server-sent events, processing database result sets, or any sequence of async values.

tokio-stream and futures::stream provide combinators like map, filter, take, merge, and more -- just like Iterator adapters but async.

The while let Some(item) = stream.next().await pattern is the standard way to consume streams.
keyPoints: Stream is the async equivalent of Iterator
Use while let Some(item) = stream.next().await to consume
tokio-stream provides adapters: map, filter, take, merge, throttle
Channel receivers can be wrapped as streams with ReceiverStream
Streams support backpressure naturally through async polling
Use StreamExt trait to get combinator methods on streams
